title: Star Wars: Outer Rim - Hotshots Expansion Overview
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0PBCtb7Va-c

Daniel (@dungeondive) has been deeper into Star Wars lately than at any point since the 70s and 80s, and that enthusiasm carries this walkthrough of **Hot Shots**, a fan-made expansion for *Star Wars: Outer Rim* by Captain Kiwi, sold on Etsy. He's upfront about the sticking point first: at roughly $150 for a printed copy, this is the most he's ever paid relative to the component count. It is not, he stresses, a necessity — that title belongs to the official *Unfinished Business* expansion. Hot Shots is spice on top for people who already love the game.

What's in the box is generous: a new travel-event deck that triggers whenever you move four or more nodes, two map tiles adding Scarif, Dathomir and Yavin 4 (plus Imperial Patrol and asteroid hazard areas), nine new characters including Din Djarin with Grogu in tow, sturdy chipboard ships like the Razor Crest, and a self-contained data bank deck cleverly numbered to avoid clashing with future official releases. The standout module is the garrison tokens — planet-bound faction combat that finally gives negative reputation some teeth.

Daniel's verdict: high quality throughout, non-essential, but a worthy indulgence if you adore Outer Rim and have the disposable income.

----

Fan-made expansions like this one live or die on that price-to-content ratio — where's your personal line for paying premium money for a game you already love?
